title: Ensure that the kube-proxy metrics service is bound to localhost

input_row.title: Ensure that the kube-proxy metrics service is bound to localhost
input_row.description: Do not bind the kube-proxy metrics port to non-loopback addresses.
input_row.rationale: kube-proxy has two APIs which provided access to information about the service and can be bound to network ports. The metrics API service includes endpoints (/metrics and /configz) which disclose information about the configuration and operation of kube-proxy. These endpoints should not be exposed to untrusted networks as they do not support encryption or authentication to restrict access to the data they provide. Impact: 3rd party services which try to access metrics or configuration information related to kube-proxy will require access to the localhost interface of the node.
input_row.audit: review the start-up flags provided to kube proxy ps -ef | grep -i kube-proxy Ensure that the --metrics-bind-address parameter is not set to a value other than 127.0.0.1. From the output of this command gather the location specified in the -- config parameter. Review any file stored at that location and ensure that it does not specify a value other than 127.0.0.1 for metricsBindAddress.
input_row.remediation: Modify or remove any values which bind the metrics service to a non-localhost address Default Value: The default value is 127.0.0.1:10249 References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube-proxy/
input_row.impact: 3rd party services which try to access metrics or configuration information related to kube-proxy will require access to the localhost interface of the node.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kube_proxy_metrics_bind_address_localhost

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all kube-proxy pods or nodes running kube-proxy (depending on deployment method: DaemonSet, static pod, or systemd service).
- For each kube-proxy instance:
  - If deployed as a DaemonSet (common in managed clusters):
    - Use kubectl to get the DaemonSet spec:  
      `kubectl -n kube-system get daemonset kube-proxy -o yaml`
    - Inspect the command/args in the DaemonSet's PodSpec for the `--metrics-bind-address` flag.
    - Confirm the value is set to `127.0.0.1:10249` or another localhost address (127.0.0.1).
    - If a config file is referenced via `--config`, extract the ConfigMap or mounted file:
      - Identify the config file path from the args (e.g., `/var/lib/kube-proxy/config.conf`).
      - If mounted from a ConfigMap, use:
        `kubectl -n kube-system get configmap <name> -o yaml`
      - Inspect the `metricsBindAddress` field in the config file; ensure it is set to `127.0.0.1:10249` or another localhost address.
  - If running as a static pod or systemd service (less common in managed clusters):
    - Use `kubectl get pods -A -o wide | grep kube-proxy` to locate pods.
    - For static pods, check the manifest in `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/` (requires host access; if not available, mark as not verifiable).
    - For systemd, this typically requires host access and would be marked Manual if not exposed via Kubernetes API.
- **Pass Criteria:** All kube-proxy instances have `--metrics-bind-address` set to `127.0.0.1:<port>` (default 10249), or the `metricsBindAddress` field in the config file is set to a localhost address.
- **Fail Criteria:** Any kube-proxy instance binds metrics to a non-localhost address (e.g., `0.0.0.0`, external IP, or `::`).

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
